hero.alt: A massive whale shark swimming gracefully in deep blue water near the surface
hero.caption: The whale shark is the largest fish in the sea, reaching lengths of up to 40 feet.

habitat.fileTitle: File:Whale shark, Rhincodon typus, at Daedalus in the Egyptian Red Sea. (35827412321).jpg
habitat.alt: A whale shark swimming through clear waters of the Red Sea near coral reefs
habitat.caption: They live in warm, tropical oceans and seas around the world.

diet.fileTitle: File:Whale Shark (Rhincodon typus) with open mouth in La Paz, Mexico.jpg
diet.alt: A whale shark feeding with its massive mouth wide open to filter plankton
diet.caption: Despite their size, whale sharks are gentle filter feeders that eat tiny plankton and krill.

baby.alt: A smaller, juvenile whale shark swimming in deep ocean waters
baby.caption: Mother whale sharks give birth to live young called pups, which are about 2 feet long at birth.

family.alt: A wild whale shark swimming in the open sea
family.caption: Whale sharks are solitary animals, but they will gather in groups where food is plentiful.

range.alt: A whale shark cruising through warm waters of the western Caribbean
range.caption: They travel huge distances across oceans to find warm water and rich food sources.

size.alt: A massive whale shark swimming alongside a small boat, demonstrating its immense size
size.caption: Whale sharks can weigh up to 20 tons — about as heavy as three school buses!

closeup.alt: Close-up of a whale shark's broad, flat head showing its unique pattern of pale spots and stripes
closeup.caption: Every whale shark has a unique pattern of spots behind its gills, like a human fingerprint.

fun-fact.alt: A whale shark feeding near the surface in shallow coastal waters
fun-fact.caption: A whale shark has about 3,000 tiny teeth, but they do not use them to eat!

core-habitat.alt: A whale shark swimming in sunlit waters near the surface
core-habitat.caption: They prefer warm tropical coastal waters and open oceans.

core-diet.alt: A whale shark sucking in water and filtering out food
core-diet.caption: They can filter over 1,500 gallons of water every hour to catch tiny prey.

core-family.alt: A whale shark gliding silently in the deep blue ocean
core-family.caption: They are gentle giants and do not pose any danger to humans.

core-baby.alt: A young juvenile whale shark near the surface
core-baby.caption: Very little is known about where baby whale sharks grow up.

core-range.alt: A whale shark migrating through the ocean
core-range.caption: They are found in warm oceans around the equator.
